original: US Surveyor 7 lands near lunar crater Tycho
John Severin sets 100-mi unicycle speed record, 9 h 21 m

NFC Championship, Candlestick Park, SF: San Francisco 49ers beat Dallas Cowboys 28-27; "The Catch" - an iconic moment in NFL history - Dwight Clark makes a fingertip catch for a TD fromJoe Montanawith 58 seconds remaining; SF goes on to win the Super Bowl
PBS (the National Educational TV) begins as a 70 station network
Petra Schneider swims world record 1500 m freestyle (15:43.31)
WKBD TV channel 50 in Detroit, Michigan (IND) begins broadcasting
Soyuz 27 launches, carrying 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 6 space station
Clara Peller 1st asks, "Where's the Beef?" in commercial for Wendy's hamburger chain
Canadian astronomers report fast radio bursts (FRBs) of probable extragalactic origin, one repeating six times at the CHIME observatory, British Columbia
Climate scientists report that 2024 is Earth's hottest year on record and the first year to pass the symbolic 1.5 °C temperature limit agreed upon at the Paris Climate Accord in 2015

LA Kings centerWayne Gretzkybecomes NHL’s all-time leading scorer in combined regular season & playoff points; 4 assists in 5-4 home win over Edmonton brings his total to 2,011, 1 more than Gordie Howe
The US Mint issues quarter coins commemorating poetMaya Angelou, first black woman to be depicted
Ukrainian bacteriologist Wademar Haffkine performs the first human trial for a vaccine for the plague on himself during the Bombay epidemic
"Mickey Mouse" & "Silly Symphony" comics syndicated
USSR's Venera 6 launched for parachute landing on Venus
NASA confirms 2024 the world's warmest year on record, topping the previous high set in 2023
Ian McKellenofficially confirms that he will reprise the role of Gandalf in "The Hobbit" film adaptations
Eruptions on Mount Huascaran in Peru destroy 7 villages & kill 3,500
World's 1st flying boat's maiden flight, (Glenn Curtiss in NY)
Norwegian speed skater Oscar Mathisen skates world 500m record in 43.7s in Oslo, Norway
US Army bounces 1st radar signal off the Moon from Ft. Monmouth, New Jersey
Maurice Richardscores his 16th career hat trick in the Montreal Canadiens' 3-0 win over NY Rangers to bring his career total to 274 goals; passes Howie Morenz as NHL's #2 all-time leading goal scorer
Elvis Presleyrecords single "Heartbreak Hotel", written by Mae Boren Axton and Tommy Durden, at RCA Studios in Nashville, Tennessee
Chandra Cheesborough, American 4x100 runner (Olympic gold 1984), born in Jacksonville, Florida
Oil discovered at Spindletop, Beaumont, marking the start of the Texas oil boom (gusher age)
Cargo ship SS Flying Enterprise sinks in the English Channel after weeks of trying to salvage it
Suffragettes, the "Silent Sentinels," first protest outside the White House in Washington, D.C., led byAlice Pauland the National Woman's Party(NWP)
US Senate proposes female suffrage
Fritz Lang's silent film "Metropolis" premieres in Berlin
New Zealand's 1st game of Test cricket is held in Christchurch, NZ. Englishman Maurice Allom takes a Test hat-trick.
RCA introduces 45 RPM record
Vee-Jay Records releases "Introducing the Beatles", the first Beatles album released in the US
Preview Center opens at Walt Disney World, Florida - first building to open
Boston guard Dana Barros sinks a 3-point field goal in his 89th straight game, during the Celtics' 113-104 win over Sacramento Kings; NBA record streak ends in next game when he goes 0 from 9
First Comet of 1997 Discovered: Comet 1997 A1
PoetsElizabeth BarrettandRobert Browningbegin corresponding
First US President to visit a foreign country in wartime -FDRleaves for Casablanca, Morocco

Kareem Abdul-Jabbarof the Los Angeles Lakers scores his 34,000th career point during 124-102 win over Indiana Pacers; only NBA player to reach the milestone at that time, holds career record until 2023
Mary Ingalls, sister of American author Laura Ingalls Wilder (Little House on the Prairie), born in Pepin County, Wisconsin (d. 1928)
Nick Cravat [Nicholas Cuccia], American actor (Twilight Zone - "Gremlin" in "Nightmare at 20,000 Feet" episode), born in New York City (d. 1994)
Jerry Wexler, American music producer who coined the term "rhythm and blues" (Aretha Franklin, Led Zeppelin, Bob Dylan), born in The Bronx, New York (d. 2008)
Max Patkin, American baseball player and clown, born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1999)
Donald Knuth, American mathematician & computer scientist, born in Milwaukee, Wisconsin
Constantinople chariot racing green and blue supporters due to be executed escape, prompting Nika revolt
Stephen III of Moldavia defeats the Ottoman Empire at the Battle of Vaslui
Frederick Gardner Cottrell, American inventor (electrostatic precipitator), born in Oakland, California (d. 1948)
First tea from leaves of indigenous plants of Assam, India, arrives in the United Kingdom [date approximate]
1st underground railway opens in London
Montreal's Newsy Lalonde scores 6 goals as Canadiens beat Toronto St. Patricks, 14-7 at Mount Royal Arena, Montreal; combined 21 goals an NHL record
"Pete the Tramp" cartoon strip by C D Russell debuts
UN General Assembly meets for the first time in London
Pirate Radio Station Free Derby begins operation by Northern Ireland
A large piece of the chalk cliff at Beachy Head collapses into the sea, near East Sussex, England
Oceans warming faster than previous thought due to fossil fuel burning, according to data published in journal "Science"
RepublicanEdward W. Brookeof Massachusetts takes his seat as the first African American popularly elected to the US Senate
Fatboy Slim's music single "Praise You" hits #1 in the UK
Charles Ingalls, father of American author Laura Ingalls Wilder (Little House on the Prairie), born in Cuba, New York (d. 1902)
Barbara Hepworth, English abstract artist, sculptor and child actress (Rescued by Rover), born in Wakefield, West Riding of Yorkshire (d. 1975)
Ray Bolger, American dancer, actor, and singer (The Wizard of Oz - "Scarecrow"; Partridge Family - "Grandpa Renfrew"), born in Dorchester, Massachusetts (d. 1987)
Robert Woodrow Wilson, American physicist and radio astronomer (1978 Nobel laureate), born in Houston, Texas
